title: Living in Boulder vlog 12 -  10 -  2017: Google Earthing Roman Buildings

enrichment.summary: Kyle records a late-night vlog after pulling an all-nighter, updating viewers on his progress building a 3D model of ancient Rome using Google Earth. He describes his organizational system of 14 regions and 40 precincts, his method for estimating building counts using historical sources, and his building-placement workflow including height and color coding for insulae and houses.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: pleased with how quickly buildings can be placed — optimistic he can finish the month strong
acknowledges he is not very well organized but is working through it incrementally

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is sitting at 36 in the morning on December 11th.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle didn't go to sleep last night and is going to bed right after doing this video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle hasn't done a video for a couple of days because he has been busy working.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle was trying to finish up the roads in his last video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is now working on building buildings instead of roads.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle went into Boulder to figure out how to do his buildings.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle created an imaginary Aralean Wall around Boulder, the same size as the one around Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle decided to have 40 precincts in Boulder to keep it consistent with Rome.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: Rome has 14 regions.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle found that region five in Rome was too big to handle as one unit, so he decided to break it out.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle organized region five into 40 precincts across 14 regions, with six precincts per region.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle traced and completed all the buildings for the first three precincts in region five in one day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle followed the pattern of region nine for his building layout.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: There are 42,000 to 46,000 buildings in Silla, Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle's house is in Rome in the third-century region he used.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle used Ashby's book, a Bible for Roman buildings, to estimate the number of buildings in region nine.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: Region nine in Rome has 2,777 buildings in the 40th house of Silla.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: Region nine is 1.70 square kilometers in size.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: Region five is almost as big as region nine, at 1.67 square kilometers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle estimated that region five would have around 2,500 buildings.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle traced half of the buildings in region five in one day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle sets buildings at 18 meters, the highest they can be.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle makes every other building 13 meters tall and colors them orange and silver.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle makes one out of every 20 buildings a house, colored red and 5 meters tall.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is pleased with the progress, as it means he can finish building by the end of the year.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle realized he needs to add more roads to his project.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle finds adding roads more fun than removing them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to finish regions five and six before moving on to the next ones.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is excited to make videos after completing his project.

claims.subject: Neil deGrasse Tyson
claims.claim: Kyle imagines a Cosmos-style show where he rides a magic carpet-like bicycle instead of a spaceship.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to go to sleep and think of more ideas.

transcript.text: Hello Living Boulder V-log. It's 8.36 in the morning on December 11th. I didn't go to sleep last night so I'm going to go to bed right after doing this. I turned myself into a nocturnalist.

I haven't done a video for a couple days. I've been too busy working and then you get tired and then you don't do it. But I'm doing it this time. So last time I did the video I think what was happening was I was like trying to finish up the roads.
So where I am now is I have finished up the roads and now I'm working on building the buildings.
So in order to figure out a way to do my buildings I went into Boulder and I looked at the area within Boulder in my imaginary Aurelian wall around Rome.
I put one around Boulder. Same size, 19 kilometers. And saw that there are I think 40 precincts in that area. And of different sizes. One of them, the business one is really big.
So then I went into Rome and I decided I'd have 40 precincts to keep it consistent. Rome has 14 regions that Augustus made.
But that was too big on this area, on this area, on this section, region 5. It was just too big to just try to do them all at one unit. So I wanted to break it up.
So I figured I'd have 40 precincts in 14 regions. So it's 6 precincts to a region. And that divides up really well.
And so on region 5, I did, I actually traced, completed all of the buildings for the first three precincts of region 5. So half of it.
What was that like one day? It's really not that time consuming. And there were, I'm going to follow the pattern of region 9.
I said this the other day, but on Wikipedia, it said there were 42,000 to 46,000 buildings or insula and houses in Rome in the 3rd century, regionaries.
But I clicked on the link for that and I didn't say where it was written anywhere. So, and I don't see how, I don't, I didn't hear any regionary from the 4th century. That's a long time ago.
So, so to figure out how many buildings there are, I decided to just do platinum and ashby. That's like the Bible for apartment buildings written 100 years ago.
And they said that in region 9, it was 2777 insula apartment buildings and 140 houses almost. And that's 1.72 square kilometers. So I think that was like 1,600 or 4,600 per square kilometer.
So just using that formula. So I forget the formula. So I just type it in from those numbers I remember from region 9.
So, but even with all those, you know, buildings, that's like, it's 1.67 square kilometers, region 5. So it's almost as big, you know, as region 9. So you're talking 2,500 buildings.

Um, but, and I did like half of that in one day. So it's really not that big of a deal, you know. One of the precincts would have like 500 buildings, but what you do is you just trace over the ones that are there and just go, and then you bat jet at them.

Um, however, I have them at 18 meters, which is the highest that they could be. And then, uh, 61 feet. And then, um, I go in and every other one, even numbered ones, I, uh, make them 14, I mean, 13 meters and then make the color orange instead of brown.
And then the houses. And then the houses. One out of every 20 is a house. Make those red at 5 meters, which is the difference between 18 and 15 meters. So, anyway, it goes by quickly.
I was very pleased. Because this means I can just build room, like, to the building this month. You know, like maybe by the end of the year, who knows.
Um, I quit because this morning, when I got to the point where I realized I had to put some more roads in. You know, like, all the roads, it's not perfect.
I, I don't realize things, and I don't want to fine tune it. So I want to add roads. Which is better than having to take them away, because it takes a while to find them.

Um, but, because you can't find them by clicking on them. But, um, and I'm not very well organized, but, so yeah. Um, but that's what I'm going to do tomorrow when I get up and build a couple more roads in there.
And then finish that. And, uh, Precinct, Precinct 4, 5, and 6, and then move on to the next one. It'll just fall like dumb, and I was mad.
And I can't wait, um, I say I can't wait to do the videos of it after. I've got all kinds of cool ideas.
I think I'm going to, Neil deGrasse Tyson has this, has his Cosmos show where he's in his little spaceship.
Well, instead of being in a spaceship, I think I'll be on a bicycle that, it's like magic carpet, it's like magic bike that can fly.
So I'll be riding my bike around a thousand feet above, and I'm like, hey, look at Rome, look at that.
You know, and then I'm like, time to descend.
Whish!
Anyway, I think I'm going to go to sleep right now and think of new ideas while I'm lying in bed.
Okay, peace out.
